name: Milton Nascimento
abstract: Milton Silva Campos do Nascimento (Brazilian Portuguese: [ˈmiwtõ ˈsiwvɐ ˈkɐ̃puz du nasiˈmẽtu]; born October 26, 1942), also known as Bituca, is a Brazilian singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist. Nascimento has recorded 32 studio albums and has won five Grammy Awards, including Best World Music Album for his album Nascimento in 1998…

concerts.label: Milton Nascimento, Singer
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Then there’s an A&M recording with Milton Nascimento. It’s got everybody in there. >> [INTERVIEWEE] Milton Nascimento, Vera Cruz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: And then later Milton Nascimento. I didn’t like it when he sang falsetto, but when he’s down in his range he’s a beautiful singer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: And of course I knew a little bit about Milton Nascimento but not as much as I did after working with Sergio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: "when I heard Milton Nascimento and people like that, in the early seventies."
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Maria Schneider
about_quotes.evidence: Milton Nascimento or something like that. ... People whose music inspires me
about_quotes.doc_title: Maria Schneider interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
